revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:automatic wikipedia redirects]]" to "<nowiki>[[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]</nowiki>"
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵↵↵" to " "
Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
Bot: wrap 6 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23388260132|commit triggered pipeline]]

[[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Demo%2Fdoc here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template redirect|Template Pre|Doc pre}} {{Template shortcut|tpre}} {{Lua|Module:Demo}} This template simply places template code (or any other code) in a {{tag|syntaxhighlight|params=lang="wikitext"}} tag and then places the rendering of that code below. {{demo|{{your template}}}} '''{{red|IMPORTANT:}}''' You must wrap {{tag|nowiki}} tags around the source to be demonstrated (or use {{t|escape}}, which has a demo-escape example), like so: {{demo|[template source]}} Failure to wrap {{tag|nowiki}} tags around the source may cause the source to be executed before it reaches this template. {{demo|{{fail}}}} produces: {{demo|{{fail}}}} === Pre style === Set {{para|style}} to add any CSS text to the {{tag|pre}} tag. For example, {{demo|style=margin-left: 1.6em;|{{done}}}} will produce: {{demo|style=margin-left: 1.6em;|{{done}}}} === Separator === You can change the separator between the {{tag|syntaxhighlight|o}} and the template by setting the {{para|sep}} parameter. {{demo|sep=produces|{{done}}}} {{demo|sep=produces|{{done}}}} You may use {{para|sep|<td>}} can make the demonstration fit in a table: {| border="1" |- |{{demo|br=|{{((}}done{{))}}}} |} Instead of using {{para|sep}}, you can also specify the number of line breaks between the code and the template in the {{para|br}} parameter. === Reverse order === Use {{para|reverse|1}} to show the code snippet and the result in reverse order: {{demo|reverse=1|{{tl|template}}}} will produce {{demo|reverse=1|{{tl|template}}}} === Category removal === For templates that add categories (e.g. {{t|Asbox}}), set {{para|nocat}} (or {{para|demo_kill_categories}}) to anything to prevent them from showing up in the output: {{demo|br=produces:|demo_kill_categories=yes |{{Asbox | image = Østfold_våpen.svg | pix = 30 | subject = [[Østfold]] location | name = Template:Østfold-geo-stub | category = Østfold geography stubs | tempsort = * }} }} === Module === If the template used is based on [[:en:Lua (programming language)|Lua]], you can pass args directly to the demonstrated module by invoking [[Module:Demo]] like so: (this would reduce server stress) {{demo| {{#invoke:Demo|module|demo_module=RoundN|demo_module_func=N2|demo_template=Round2 |bold_winner=high |May 1|Team A|1|Team B|2 }}}} Note: It will attempt to preserve line breaks for unnamed params, but such is impossible for named params since those are stripped of whitespace/order before being passed to the module. Also note, you should use {{para|demo_sep}} instead of {{para|sep}} (all args you don't want being passed should be prefixed with demo_ if not already) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Content", "description": "must be wrapped in " }, "sep": { "description": "Number of line breaks, or wikitext to fill in as the linebreak" }, "nocat": { "type": "boolean", "aliases": [ "demo_kill_categories" ] }, "result_arg": {}, "style ": { "label": " style" }, "reverse": { "label": "Reverse order", "description": "Show the code snippet and the result in reverse order", "type": "boolean", "default": "0", "autovalue": "1" } } } == See also== * {{tl|Demo-inline}}, for an inline version of this template * {{tl|Escape}} - which has an example of this template without nowiki tags * {{tl|Nowiki template demo}} which uses [[Module:Template test case]] * {{tl|Automarkup}} which uses [[Module:Automarkup]] ===Other formatting templates=== {{#lsth:Template:Template-linking_templates|Other formatting templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Documentation assistance templates]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Template test cases| ]] [[Category:Wikipedia formatting templates]] [[Category:Wikitext processing templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24926181992|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{Template redirect|Template Pre|Doc pre}} {{Template shortcut|tpre}} {{Lua|Module:Demo}} This template simply places template code (or any other code) in a {{tag|syntaxhighlight|params=lang="wikitext"}} tag and then places the rendering of that code below. {{demo|{{your template}}}} '''{{red|IMPORTANT:}}''' You must wrap {{tag|nowiki}} tags around the source to be demonstrated (or use {{t|escape}}, which has a demo-escape example), like so: {{demo|[template source]}} Failure to wrap {{tag|nowiki}} tags around the source may cause the source to be executed before it reaches this template. {{demo|{{fail}}}} produces: {{demo|{{fail}}}} === Pre style === Set {{para|style}} to add any CSS text to the {{tag|pre}} tag. For example, {{demo|style=margin-left: 1.6em;|{{done}}}} will produce: {{demo|style=margin-left: 1.6em;|{{done}}}} === Separator === You can change the separator between the {{tag|syntaxhighlight|o}} and the template by setting the {{para|sep}} parameter. {{demo|sep=produces|{{done}}}} {{demo|sep=produces|{{done}}}} You may use {{para|sep|<td>}} can make the demonstration fit in a table: {| border="1" |- |{{demo|br=|{{((}}done{{))}}}} |} Instead of using {{para|sep}}, you can also specify the number of line breaks between the code and the template in the {{para|br}} parameter. === Reverse order === Use {{para|reverse|1}} to show the code snippet and the result in reverse order: {{demo|reverse=1|{{tl|template}}}} will produce {{demo|reverse=1|{{tl|template}}}} === Category removal === For templates that add categories (e.g. {{t|Asbox}}), set {{para|nocat}} (or {{para|demo_kill_categories}}) to anything to prevent them from showing up in the output: {{demo|br=produces:|demo_kill_categories=yes |{{Asbox | image = Østfold_våpen.svg | pix = 30 | subject = [[Østfold]] location | name = Template:Østfold-geo-stub | category = Østfold geography stubs | tempsort = * }} }} === Module === If the template used is based on [[:en:Lua (programming language)|Lua]], you can pass args directly to the demonstrated module by invoking [[Module:Demo]] like so: (this would reduce server stress) {{demo| {{#invoke:Demo|module|demo_module=RoundN|demo_module_func=N2|demo_template=Round2 |bold_winner=high |May 1|Team A|1|Team B|2 }}}} Note: It will attempt to preserve line breaks for unnamed params, but such is impossible for named params since those are stripped of whitespace/order before being passed to the module. Also note, you should use {{para|demo_sep}} instead of {{para|sep}} (all args you don't want being passed should be prefixed with demo_ if not already) == TemplateData == {{TemplateData header}} { "params": { "1": { "label": "Content", "description": "must be wrapped in " }, "sep": { "description": "Number of line breaks, or wikitext to fill in as the linebreak" }, "nocat": { "type": "boolean", "aliases": [ "demo_kill_categories" ] }, "result_arg": {}, "style ": { "label": " style" }, "reverse": { "label": "Reverse order", "description": "Show the code snippet and the result in reverse order", "type": "boolean", "default": "0", "autovalue": "1" } } } == See also== * {{tl|Demo-inline}}, for an inline version of this template * {{tl|Escape}} - which has an example of this template without nowiki tags * {{tl|Nowiki template demo}} which uses [[Module:Template test case]] * {{tl|Automarkup}} which uses [[Module:Automarkup]] ===Other formatting templates=== {{#lsth:Template:Template-linking_templates|Other formatting templates}} {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Documentation assistance templates]] [[Category:Programming typing-aid templates]] [[Category:Template test cases| ]] [[Category:Wikipedia formatting templates]] [[Category:Wikitext processing templates]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
